tokens: I'm ready to go over the sill.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Find the LEC; hey, come on, babe.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pull the door all the way back.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me hand you something (probably either the jettison bag or the LEC).
(Pause) When you go out, and then I'll hand it to you.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Bye-bye; see you in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Am I hanging on something here?
I get the feeling I'm stuck under something here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: All right; now you're in good shape.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Go straight down from where you are.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You're headed right square out the hatch.
You'll have to bend over more, though.
Move to your right, you're...There you are.
(Pause) You got to kneel down a little more.
(Pause) Well, I'll push you (down) if you don't mind.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Got this garbage bag in my way.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) Okay; I'm out on the porch.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay; let me pull a pip pin (holding the D-ring on the MESA release).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Man, that's a heck of a tug with that handle.
(I'll go) down another step (on the ladder).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: While you're doing that, let me get the LEC ready for you.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That handle's in there (in its mounting bracket) like something I never saw before.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Was it easy to get...Can you get the lock out?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I couldn't even get the handle out of the deal.
I just pulled the cable; the MESA's down.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You haven't got anything to do, so you can take this (probably either the
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, I'll tell you what we're parked next to.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We're about 25 feet in front of the Surveyor Crater.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That's where we wanted to be.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I bet you when I get down to the bottom of the ladder, I can see the Surveyor.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, guy; you want to take this (end of the LEC) with you, Pete?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay; now hold her there a second, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Do you have any TV, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: No; I'm at the top of the ladder.
Now look, this thing is all the way out of the bag.
How do you want me to do it?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: No, but this thing isn't all the way out of the...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Here; let me have this end of it.
Let me come back up the ladder a notch.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Which end (of the LEC) is that?
This is the end I want.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Sure flies at one-sixth, doesn't it?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Looks like we got 900 feet of this stuff!
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I've got to get my camera on you, babe.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I can't go down yet, anyhow.
- get the LEC all the way down.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Still can't figure out what kind of snarl I've got here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Can you look out your window?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: All right; I think I see what's wrong.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Oh; that LEC came out of the bag in three pieces and, as you would might well imagine, I picked the wrong piece.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Do you want me to pull it back in and throw you the end?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Man, they aren't kidding when they say things get dusty.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me get the old (70 mm Hasselblad) camera on you, babe.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Man, is that a pretty looking sight, that LM.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You're coming into the picture now, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Okay; got the old (16 mm sequence) camera running.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Man, that may have been a small one for Neil, but that's a long one for me.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'm going to step off the pad.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Guess what I see sitting on the side of the crater!
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It can't be any further than 600 feet from here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I've got a little chore (the contingency sample) to do here, right?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I say that was well planned, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Just a couple of months (of planning and training) with a lot of people.
(Reading) "Deploy the LEC and the MESA." That's done.
I'm looking at my mobility; c.g.
(Moving off camera to the right) I have the decided impression I don't want to move too rapidly, but I can walk quite well.
The Surveyor really is sitting on the side of a steep slope, I'll tell you that.
Now I'll work on my contingency sample.
(Pause) Got to walk real careful, Al.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (To Al) Can you see me all right?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Long Pause) (To Houston) As you might suspect from some of the pictures Neil brought back, gang, I have several small rocks sitting out in front of me that have a neat amount of dirt built up around them.
I'm not sure my descent engine didn't blow 'em there.
But then again, it may not have.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Is the dirt built up on the side closest to the LM?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Well, let me...I'm going over to get my contingency sample, and I'll get one of the rocks in the sample.
And yeah, as a matter of fact, it is built up on the side that (pause) the LM landed on.
Let me get a...(Pause) Well, there's one scoop.
And another with some more rocks in it.
This dirt's just like the one-sixth-g airplane, Al...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: ...(chuckling) and you get to chase it around.
Now, I'll tell you...You know, this Sun...It really is...It's just like somebody's got a super-bright spotlight.
Here's another good-looking rock - whoops - in the sample.
(Pause) Here's another rock I want to get in it.
(Pause) I think that's about enough, don't you?
Except there's one big rock that's too pretty to pass up.
No; I may not be a hog.
I'll go over here and get this other one, though.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Boy, you sure lean forward, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, "lean forward": I feel like I'm going to fall over in any direction.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Say, Houston; one of the first things that I can see, by golly, is little glass beads.
I got a piece about a quarter of an inch in sight, and I'm going to put it in the contingency sample bag, if I can get it.
Am I really leaning over, Al?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: On Earth, you'd fall over, I believe.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: On Earth, you'd fall over leaning that far forward.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It seems a little weird, I'll tell you.
Don't think you're going to steam around here quite as fast as you thought you were.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'll tell you, your boots are digging in the soil quite a bit.
If you don't pick up your feet, you really kick a load of dirt ahead of you.
Your left foot's got a big mound ahead of it right now that it's just pushing along.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Uh-oh, did I hear a tone?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Yeah; I've got an H2O A (flag).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Feedwater's on and it's still real cool in here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: What do you think I may have done?
Broken through the sublimator or something?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We have a good shot of you there (on the TV), Pete.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Well I'm starting to take this baby (the MESA thermal blanket) apart.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: The descent engine, it's just like Neil's.
I didn't dig any crater at all!
(Pause) Al, you've really got to watch your step down here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Long Pause while Pete removes the thermal blankets from the MESA)
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Look at all those good things
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: The MESA's too low for once.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Al, how's the feedwater look now?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Well, it's still got an 'A' in there (in the warning flag window on the top of the
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, we would like to watch it a little bit.
It could be instrumentation (that is, a sensor malfunction).
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: How long have I been out, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, you've been out 25 minutes, and you're about 4 minutes ahead (of the planned timeline).
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I got the table out, adjusted the MESA (to a comfortable working height), and I'm setting up ETB at this time.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) Hey, Al, you can work out here all day.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Long Pause) Dum dee dum dum dum.
(Pause) I think our next big surprise, Al, is getting this thing (the ETB) up (to the cabin with the LEC).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Oh, it still shows an A, but it's cool.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Just beginning to warm up to this task.
(Long Pause) LCG (Liquid Cooled Garment) water pump sounds like a diesel truck running out here.
Comforting to know that it's running.
I'm off to get the (PLSS replacement) battery(s).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I think I know what happened, Houston.
I think I know what happened.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Making a rare mis-identification) Pete, go ahead.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: What did you just do, Al?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Man, I just figured it out.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You just blew water out the front of the cabin.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That's what had happened to the PLSS.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Oh, the door had swung shut, like it did before; and probably bothered the sublimator, 'cause it wasn't in a good vacuum anymore.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: So I opened the door and it's probably going to start working in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: When you opened the door, that thing shot iceballs (laughing) straight out the hatch.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: There's probably all from my...Never thought you'd have to do that.
Hey, you bent the outside of that front hatch on the way out.
You tore some of the skin.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Must have hit your PLSS there.
It's got a nice scrape mark right along the way out.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I didn't realize that the hatch could close quite so tightly like that, because when I was working on the other side (Pete's side) of the cabin, the hatch went closed and I didn't notice it.
And apparently it quit holding a good vacuum in here.
My H2O 'A' flag is off, now, so everything is copasetic.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It (meaning Al's PLSS telemetry) looks good down here.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I've got both (PLSS LiOH replacement) canisters, Al; both batteries.
As soon as I get them in here (in the ETB), I got to pack the contingency sample.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Sitting tight, waiting for you, babe.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I tell you, you really can't move as fast as I thought you could.
You got to take it real easy.
Get the feeling that I'm most spiffy on the balance up here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Oh, man, did I get dirt all over myself.
(Pause) This is what is known as dirt dirt.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me know when you start heading back out there (west of the ladder) to do the ETB, Pete.
I'll get a good shot of you, babe (with the 16-mm camera, as per
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Getting ready to do it in a second, Al.
Just as soon as I get the bag, (the ETB, hooked to the LEC).
I got the contingency sample in the bag.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I've got everything else: PLSS batteries, (PLSS) LiOH canisters, and I just got to hook up (the ETB to) the LEC.
Bo bo bo bo; dee dee; dee.
(Pause) I tell you one thing, we're going to be a couple of dirty boogers.
Why don't you take up a little slack, Al?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I tell you, this is dirt dirt.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: My end of the ETB (means "LEC") just came out of its metal slot.
Somebody that made this pin didn't make it like all the training units; they made it littler, so it came out.
I caught it as it was going by.
You know this metal pin that keeps it from sliding all the way through?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Unfortunately, it's smaller than the metal holder.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Wait; wait; wait; wait; wait; wait.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) Okay; now...Hold the phone a second.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Hearty laughter) Wait until I get in this shadow.
Because I can't see what I'm doing looking right into the Sun.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'm about to fall down this little crater hole.
Boy, it really does get...I gotta get over here in the shadow.
I'm down in a - oops - another crater hole.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It's a regular obstacle course over there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Man, am I going to get dirty!
(Pause) Tell me if it (meaning the LEC)'s clear of the porch rail, huh?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I can't see a thing, looking into the Sun.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Moving over into the LM shadow) That a boy; now I can see.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'll have this stuff right back out to you in a flash.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: While you're doing that, what was I supposed to do?
Oh, I know: "Possible TV deploy." I'll go work on the, yeah, (pause) tripod.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Dum dum, da dee da dee dum.
Trying to learn to move faster.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: How long we been out, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, you're 34 minutes into the EVA; and you're right on the nominal timeline.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (Pause) I may have filled the bag too full.
(Pause) Oh, I know what it is (that's keeping the TV camera from coming loose).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Putting the Hasselblads in the ETB) One camera; two cameras.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Straining a little as he tries to remove the locking pins that hold the TV camera in the MESA) (Here) comes one TV camera.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I've got to stop what I'm doing.
Let me come over here and get it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Take it away whenever you need it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: If I fall over I can ...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me get over to this side.
(Moving out of the picture to his left) Let's get it over the handrail.
Hold it, hold it right there.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now, I can't see it on account of the Sun, so tell me when it's over the handrail.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now, just hold it right there.
Hold it; hold it; hold it.
(Removing the cameras from the hanging bag) One...That is - dad-da-dee-dee - two.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Reading his checklist) "LEC; possible TV deploy; LMP egress; contingency sample area (photos); deploy color chart and place 70 millimeter (camera) on the MESA." So I get me a camera (to photograph Al coming out of the LM).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Got to set the (sequence) camera, and I'll be right out.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me know so I can photograph you.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (To Gibson) We sampled in quite a few places, Houston, so I'm taking a bunch of pictures.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) Pete, for your information for those photos, your shadow length right now is about 45 feet on a level plane.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: "Deploy the color chart (on an undisturbed surface)" Ho ho.
(Pause) Hey, I'm learning to do it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pete belches) Houston, how does the LM look?
I'm getting ready to go out the front door.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: No way I'm gonna...I wonder if I can get in the bottom of this crater hole?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, you're at 40 minutes into the timeline, and you're about 4 minutes ahead.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Got to run through this (small) crater.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, you might want to give me some directions, too.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You're coming straight out and the further you can bend over the better.
All right; move to your right.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) That's it; get your knees down.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay; I'm pulling the hatch closed here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, you're right at the edge of the porch.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Man, if I'd landed 20 feet behind where I landed, we'd have landed right smack in that (Surveyor) crater.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Oh, it's kind of hard to move the door.
I was just getting in and trying to get it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (To Pete) I'm going to try to keep the door open for us, there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pretty good; I'd better get my visor down though.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Boy, the LM looks nice on the outside.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Houston, let me ask you a question.
How important is that color chart?
I tried to spike it in the ground, you know, so it was perpendicular to the Sun; and it just didn't do that, and it's all covered with dirt.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I can go back and re-salvage it, if you want to take the time.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Press on with what you're doing there, Pete, and we'll get an answer back to you.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: ...turn around and give me a big smile.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Reading) "Place (70 mm camera on MESA)"...Wait a minute.
"Deploy color chart on undisturbed surface." Didn't make it.
"Contingency sample area (photos)" I got, and "LMP egress" I got.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Moving slowly off camera to the right ( roughly north);
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Yeah, you really do begin to adapt.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: If you hop it a little bit...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: If you turn around and walk over to your right a little bit and look over that crater, you're going to see our pal (Surveyor III) sitting there.
And that's one steep slope it's on.
(Pause) Okay; now what have you got all over your boot?
You picked up a piece of landing gear insulation.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, I'm going after (the S-band antenna)...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, you've got to watch it in these shadows.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You can't see what you're doing.
Come over here where I am.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Will you look how close we almost landed to that crater!
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It didn't even dig a hole!
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Stops to answer Gibson) Go ahead, Houston.
We've got to save this (garbled under Gibson)...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, will you give us status on the LM and also some comments on your boot penetration?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: My comments are exactly the same as Neil and Buzz.
In fact, every time I get down in one of these little craters, I sink in a lot further.
I'd say our footsteps are sinking in...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: What do you want to do with it (the S-Band cover)?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Put it over by the Y-gear (the north landing gear).
I think I cover that rock box with it later.
Remember, (it was a) last minute change.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I think it's pretty much the same as Neil and Buzz found, don't you, Al?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: One thing I've noticed: it seems to compact into a very shiny surface (where you step on the surface).
I guess the particles are very small and very cohesive; so every boot print, as you look at it, looks almost like it's a piece of rubber itself.
It's so well defined, you can't see any grains in it or anything.
transcript.speaker: Bean
